title: chrysothamnus parryi subsp. affinis
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi subsp. affinis, also known as the pygmy rabbitbrush, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Chrysothamnus parryi subsp. affinis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
